paper_metadata.title: Voting on the environment: Price or ideology? Evidence from Swiss referendums
paper_metadata.empirical_scope_or_sample: 2,826 Swiss communes; municipal-level approval rates for three 2000 federal referendums.
exact_tax_terms_used: tax on fossil and nuclear energy
paper_metadata.carbon_tax_relevance_summary: Uses municipal referendum returns to study support for three Swiss 2000 taxes on fossil/non-renewable energy, including one proposal with revenue recycling through lower social security contributions.

determinants.definition: Share of votes for left and green parties in the 1999 national election.
determinants.mechanism: Proxy for ideological support for state intervention, redistribution, and environmental action.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Municipal approval of the 2000 Swiss referendums on taxes on fossil/non-renewable energy.
determinants.evidence_quote: the percentage of votes gained by left and green parties is strongly positive and highly statistically significant
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Positive and highly significant for all three tax votes in the full model.

determinants.definition: Average communal acceptance rate of the five previous national environmental ballots.
determinants.mechanism: Captures habitual pro-environmental voting and local social norms favoring green policies.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Municipal approval of the 2000 Swiss referendums on taxes on fossil/non-renewable energy.
determinants.evidence_quote: average result of the last five nation-wide environmental ballots ... positive and the marginal effect is relatively strong
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Positive and highly significant for all three tax votes in the full model.

determinants.mechanism: Younger communes were less supportive in these referendum outcomes.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Municipal approval of the 2000 Swiss referendums on taxes on fossil/non-renewable energy.
determinants.evidence_quote: The coefficient for young voters has a negative impact and is highly significant for all three proposals.
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Negative and highly significant across all three tax votes.

determinants.definition: Share of residents aged 60 and over.
determinants.mechanism: Older communes appear less willing to bear the taxes' costs.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Municipal approval of the 2000 Swiss referendums on taxes on fossil/non-renewable energy.
determinants.evidence_quote: For older voters the picture looks similar: ... coefficients are all negative.
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Negative for all three tax votes; significant for two proposals.

determinants.definition: Share of the population with higher education.
determinants.mechanism: May proxy longer horizons and lower discounting of future environmental benefits.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Municipal approval of the 2000 Swiss referendums on taxes on fossil/non-renewable energy.
determinants.evidence_quote: the proportion of the population with a higher education is confirmed to have a strong impact on the decision.
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Positive and highly significant across the three tax votes.

determinants.definition: Share using a car as main work-related commuting mode.
determinants.mechanism: Higher expected fuel-price burden lowers support for the tax proposals.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Municipal approval of the 2000 Swiss referendums on taxes on fossil/non-renewable energy.
determinants.evidence_quote: use of a personal car as main means to commute has a strong negative impact on approval rates.
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Negative and highly significant across all three tax votes.

determinants.definition: Average disposable income per taxpayer, with a quadratic term.
determinants.mechanism: Ability to bear tax costs; relationship is nonlinear.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Municipal approval of the 2000 Swiss referendums on taxes on fossil/non-renewable energy.
determinants.evidence_quote: inverted u-shaped relationship between the average communal income and the acceptance of the projects
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Positive at low income levels, negative at high income levels; turning point around CHF 18k-20k for two proposals.

determinants.definition: Share of women in the commune.
determinants.mechanism: May proxy gender-linked pro-social attitudes, but effect is not robust after ideology controls.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Municipal approval of the 2000 Swiss referendums on taxes on fossil/non-renewable energy.
determinants.evidence_quote: the difference between men and women vanishes once ideological variables are introduced
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Positive in the price-only model; weak/non-robust after ideology controls.
determinants.notes: Effect not robust in full model.

determinants.definition: Share of the commune that is French-speaking.
determinants.mechanism: Captures regional cultural and informational context that may shape environmental attitudes.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Municipal approval of the 2000 Swiss referendums on taxes on fossil/non-renewable energy.
determinants.evidence_quote: The coefficient for the French-speaking proportion variable is negative and highly significant.
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Negative in the price-only model; signs differ across proposals in the full model.
determinants.notes: Specification-dependent across the three tax votes.

determinants.definition: Dummy for communes with more than 20,000 inhabitants.
determinants.mechanism: Urban residents may rely less on private mobility and value environmental quality more.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Municipal approval of the 2000 Swiss referendums on taxes on fossil/non-renewable energy.
determinants.evidence_quote: the coefficients for urbanity are all positive and statistically significant, although with a small effect
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Positive in the price-only model; cancelled out after ideology controls.
determinants.notes: Effect disappears in the full model.

determinants.definition: Dummy for communes in the canton of Graubünden.
determinants.mechanism: Regional context linked to tourism and hydropower interests.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Municipal approval of the 2000 Swiss referendums on taxes on fossil/non-renewable energy.
determinants.evidence_quote: communes in this canton were on average between 6-11% more favorable to the proposals
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Positive and highly significant for all three tax votes.

determinants.definition: Share of workers in fossil-energy industries.
determinants.mechanism: Workers in exposed sectors may oppose higher energy prices due to expected output and employment losses.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Municipal approval of the 2000 Swiss referendums on taxes on fossil/non-renewable energy.
determinants.evidence_quote: regions with a larger proportion of workers in the sectors of fossil energy ... opposed the three proposals more strongly
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Negative, strongest for the green tax reform.

determinants.definition: Share of workers in the electricity sector.
determinants.mechanism: Employment exposure to higher energy prices reduces support for the tax proposals.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Municipal approval of the 2000 Swiss referendums on taxes on fossil/non-renewable energy.
determinants.evidence_quote: regions with a larger proportion of workers in the sectors of ... electricity ... opposed the three proposals more strongly
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Negative and highly significant for the solar and green tax votes.

determinants.definition: Share of workers in paper production.
determinants.mechanism: Energy-intensive industrial exposure increases perceived cost burden.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Municipal approval of the 2000 Swiss referendums on taxes on fossil/non-renewable energy.
determinants.evidence_quote: regions with a larger proportion of workers in the sectors of ... paper production ... opposed the three proposals more strongly
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Negative in both specifications; significant for the solar and energy-conservation votes.
